# GC-03: Agent Provenance Registry

**Track:** Governance Controls (`GC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Maintain a centralised registry of all agent contributions across the lifecycle: what each agent produced, which decisions it made autonomously, and which humans reviewed and approved its output. In Stage 3, tag all code with immutable provenance metadata identifying whether each contribution was written by a human, generated by an agent, or co-authored. Supports DORA third-party oversight and EU AI Act deployer obligations.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 0 | Cross-Cutting | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Register every contribution, decision, and approval in the provenance registry automatically. In Stage 3, tag all code with provenance metadata at the point of generation. |
| **Human does** | Review provenance records and reports during audits; confirm attribution accuracy; provide regulatory evidence of agent oversight. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Agent provenance record | registry_entry | Per-contribution entry recording agent identity, action taken, output reference, and approving human. | 7 years (DORA Art. 28) |
| Code provenance record | registry_entry | Per-contribution entry attributing code to human, agent, or co-authored (Stage 3). | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Compliance Officer |

Code without provenance tags cannot be included in a pull request.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 28 | Third-party ICT service provider oversight and accountability |
| DORA | Art. 8(6) | ICT documentation including agent and automation contributions; traceability of ICT changes including identification of all contributors |
| EU AI Act | Art. 17(1)(f) | Data management and quality management documentation of AI system contributions |
| EU AI Act | Art. 26(5) | Deployer obligations for monitoring AI system operation |
| EU AI Act | Art. 12 | Record-keeping requirements for high-risk AI systems including output attribution |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Governance & Compliance · **Owner role:** AI Governance Lead
